question: Which of the following is true about supracondvlar fracture of humerus?

options.A: Distal segment is dislocated anteriorly more than posterior
options.B: Cubitus valgus more common than cubitus varus during malunion
options.C: Nerve injury related manifestations are transitory
options.D: Injury causes weakness of elbow flexion

history.message:
Agent1 Round 1: Supracondylar fractures of the humerus typically involve displacement of the distal fragment, which tends to be displaced posterolaterally rather than simply anteriorly. The pattern of nerve injuries associated with these fractures can sometimes lead to persistent symptoms due to potential neuropraxia or avulsion injuries.
